plant_context.disease: Cercospora leaf spot Gray leaf spot

seed.symptoms: Rectangular gray to tan lesions with distinct borders running parallel to leaf veins; lesions may coalesce killing large leaf areas.
seed.causes: Fungus Cercospora zeae-maydis; overwinters in crop residue; favored by warm, humid conditions and extended dew periods.
seed.treatment: Foliar fungicides (strobilurins, triazoles) applied at or before tasseling.
seed.prevention: Rotate crops; till residue; plant resistant hybrids; use reduced tillage cautiously in high-risk areas.
